dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear my favored Aspect of Revaal! The Cyclops truly is in for it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: The Aspect of the Goddess Hel... I shall enjoy bearing witness to her power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: The Moon is full and getting in my eyes... on such an eve, I envy you, Lord Polyphemus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I was right in the middle of a new concoction, but I'd abandon anything for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: There really is quite a distinct aroma here, between the corpses and the shepherd's flock...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: My poisons are for mortal use, but also work on goddesses.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's have another brush with death now, sorceress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Come demonstrate to us your knowledge of the necromantic arts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Great Hecate requires me to test your wherewithal, if you would be so kind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not hesitate to draw some of your blood here, sorceress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: All my research has made me welcome this as a refreshing change of pace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I have already cursed you, sorceress! Let's see you break the spell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Careful, lest I cut that tender throat of yours like that of a mewling babe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Sacrifices must be made on nights such as this. Yours or mine ought to suffice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: As blood is shortly to be drawn, rest easy knowing not one drop shall go to waste.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Know that your youth does not entitle you to mercy, sorceress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, sorceress! Seek vengeance now for what we did to you before...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I heed the summons of the will of Night. Indeed, I'm more than happy to oblige.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: You spoke the Vow of Rivals, so as mortals sometimes say: You asked for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: My craft can kill most anything in moments, but not you. It may prove quite unpleasant though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: How is it that you're so resistant to my curses, sorceress...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well, Lord Polyphemus. Perhaps we might catch up some more some other eve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: When you invoke the will of Night, you'd best not flinch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you, sorceress! Infusing Fear into the night lets me conduct certain experiments I normally cannot. Though I'm also bound to test you here, as does Great Hecate below.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Test me! Lady Medea, what of the massive Cyclops already barring my path? He'll try to eat us both!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah. This witch here's not exactly to my taste, and besides... we got ourselves a little arrangement now. Since I can't smell as easy what with all the bodies popping up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Your fight is with the Cyclops still! My aim is merely to enact the will of Night in my own way. Do try your best to pretend I'm not even here, which may be difficult, admittedly...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you, sorceress! Infusing Fear into the night lets me conduct certain experiments I normally cannot. Though I'm also bound to test you here, as does Great Hecate below.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea! I thought you were supposed to be conducting research. And what do you mean, test me? That massive Cyclops already barring my path will try to eat us both!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah. This witch here's not exactly to my taste, and besides... we got ourselves a little arrangement now. Since I can't smell as easy what with all the bodies popping up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Your fight is with the Cyclops still! My aim is merely to enact the will of Night in my own way. Do try your best to pretend I'm not even here, which may be difficult, admittedly...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Are you quite serious, Cyclops? The bodies of the dead ought not to be consumed; all sorts of diseases and the like!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm telling you, they can be pretty good! Just got to age 'em right. But anyways we can pick up on that later. Got a warm one over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea, I cannot believe the two of you are getting on like this. The Testament of Night requires that you form alliances with those who stand in our way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: We merely have a common interest, sorceress! The Cyclops yet stands in your way, so have at him! I'm here to make the night more foul at your own behest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Where'd that one witch go, and all the bodies, huh? And just as I was starting to like the new aroma around here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You and all of us are subject to the will of Night, Cyclops. Lady Medea was drawn here for that reason alone. For my part, I find it easier not having to contend with you both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You want things easy, then what are you even doing over here? You just passed through one of the hardest towns I know, and things are only gonna get rougher.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You fixed it, meat! This witch is back, there's bodies everywhere! I've had it good. But you're about to get it bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea, aren't you supposed to stand with me here rather than with him? Though, the will of Night ought never to be questioned, and this is all part of the Vows I spoke...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Night brings terror, sorceress... a fear of the impossible and the unknown. I merely am fulfilling my responsibility unto that cause, so please! Address Lord Polyphemus here, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Lord Polyphemus. Think I like the sound of that! After all, lords tend to have big appetites. Now come on over and I'll add you to the corpses on the ground.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Even with all the extra bodies here, I can always sniff you out. You're on my turf, and you'll address me as Lord Polyphemus from now on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You can't be serious. You're not entitled to a greater status just because you asked! Or has Lady Medea poisoned your mind with newfound delusions?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't look at me, sorceress. I'm here by will of Night alone, sworn to a Vow of Silence... even though it's difficult to keep.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: She don't want to talk to you, she said, but you keep on tryin'. And you know what? I don't want to talk to you, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm telling ya, it feels funny. Itches maybe, burns a little, I don't know. You sure it's gonna help me with my sleep?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Well for mortals, it induces an eternal sleep, preceded by a pain beyond compare. I made no more assurances, and... come to think? This may not be the remedy for you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Least it hasn't messed up my sense of smell too much. 'Cause otherwise I wouldn't know we got ourselves somebody else who probably could use your remedies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff, Sniff> Eugh, my damn nose is all stuffed up. Think I'm allergic to something you got on you there, witch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: You're resistant to such toxins as could kill the strongest ox, Lord Polyphemus. Perhaps you've an aversion to the sorceress whom we must fight?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff> What, she's here? Ugh, I'm losin' it if I can't even smell 'er when she's right in front of me like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff, Sniff> Well what am I supposed to do on nights like this? If I can't even smell her, I don't know where I'm supposed to kick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Polyphemus, your nasal passages must be utterly congested. The one to whom you're referring is right there! Next time, I shall have to unearth something you can use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff> All right, sounds good, it's really not so bad. It's just if I can't smell my food, then I can't properly enjoy my meal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Inhale> Yeah, you know I think that might have done the trick! 'Cause I can smell every last corpse a couple fields away. You're all right, witch! What'd you even give me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's just... I'd stashed away old herbal remedies my aunt had given me. Thought I would never find a use for them, but... didn't have the heart to throw them out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that was awful generous of you, though now I'm in the mood for a quick bite. Or maybe a quick nap? You want to stick around?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: ...At any rate, Lord Polyphemus, we oughtn't even be speaking thus, for that is not my charge. But should you require any other remedies or toxins, I'm not far.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea, you'd consort with Polyphemus there even without the Oath of the Unseen taking effect? I can't believe this! He's repeatedly attempted to devour me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Watch with the attitude there, meat. The witch just said that she don't want to talk. You ever got somethin' to say here, you say it to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Such appalling silence in these outskirts! I've grown too much accustomed to the bustle of the city, and its lingering scents of blood and decay.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea, does Headmistress even know what you're doing here? She hadn't mentioned you'd be testing me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Great Hecate knows all. But now that you are Matron of the Oath, the consequences are yours to decide. And tonight, you spoke the Vows! Long have I been dying to oblige...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I to merely be a shadow in the dark...? I cannot help but intervene not just with actions here but words. Lord Polyphemus, talk to her, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure, whatever you say there, witch. Let me go ahead and give this piece of meat I smell here a real talking-to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: If I might briefly break my Vow of Silence, you look simply terrible, sorceress! More dead than alive. And now the Cyclops and I have to push you closer to the brink.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I had some difficulties in Ephyra on the way, though thank you for noticing. I know the will of Night demands you show no mercy to me here; anything but.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't think the will of Night ought be your chief concern... Lord Polyphemus, he is positively salivating. The power of the scent of blood! Particularly yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Those accursed curing pools there in the corners of the field... the Mandragora thrives in bloodied soil, but in doing so, it filters toxins out. How rather fortunate for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your toxins are so potent, they immediately make me feel as though I'm burning up, even with those curing pools nearby.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say? I try! You'll even find that, no matter how many times you take ill, you won't become resistant... let alone immune.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: My curses and concoctions are quite lethal normally. But you've been able to withstand them! I don't know whether to be impressed with you, or disappointed in myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought this was to be a test of my abilities and growth! It more appears to be a test of your latest experiments.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Must it merely be one or the other? Great Hecate has taught us always to be learning something more. That way, we shall grow as strong as the deadliest of poisons...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, sorceress! You're utterly committed to the Oath. As your fight remains with Lord Polyphemus, I'm to be mostly silent henceforth; though I have no wish to be rude.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you're here because of certain obligations, Lady Medea, so I certainly bear no ill will. Well not to you, at least, but to Lord Polyphemus, I am not so sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Now he's upset again. Please understand that I am not responsible for any actions that the Cyclops takes... I hardly am responsible for mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I quite like it here! As much as I've enjoyed experimenting in my little corner of the city, a bit of the outdoors is giving me some benefit, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If only I could say the same for your various toxins, venoms, poisons, and such, Lady Medea. Doesn't the will of Night forbid the use of certain substances?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that so...? Perhaps I'm not entirely caught up on all the latest bylaws; but you have bigger problems here than a bit of sour blood. Oh, Cyclops...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You went through my old barn the other night while I was out. Coulda cased the joint but you didn't. Drank from my well and that was it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm no thief. And besides, your residence is filthy! You could stand to clean it up instead of facing me. Let me know if you need assistance, as I'll soon be passing through again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah, thanks. If it was tidy in there I couldn't sniff my way around. And also, you shut up! I don't go tellin' you the way you ought to live!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Each time you know I'm here, no matter the softness of my approach or anything I do to mask my scent. Your senses are very keen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well, when you lose your eye... you got to learn to compensate. And I never saw too good to start with. Could never tell how far away things were.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It must have been extraordinarily difficult, nevertheless. And now you use these heightened abilities to tend your flock...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Heightened abilities, I like that. Look, this world's a real ugly place. Difference between you and me is, I don't got to look at it no more. Now, hold still or start running... I don't even care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I can always hear you storming through town all the way from this calm relaxing pasture. Even before my sheep get spooked.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, I figured the boulders raining down back there weren't landing near me by coincidence. You have excellent hearing, and rather good aim.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, thanks. And you got excellent taste, I'm sure. Now hold still, so I can practice my rather good aim some more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey what's your beef with Chronos, anyways? He wants to change stuff up a bit, so what? Life's hard up here. It could be easier.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It won't be. That town back there, as you call it, would have been bustling with mortals. Now it's a festering necropolis. All thanks to the Titan.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: My sheep, they're glad to be alive. No complaints. But individuals like you? You got too many ideals. Best I eat you, and be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So what does Chronos offer you exactly for these ineffectual attempts to bar my passage?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't see how that's any of your business, meat. You're the one barging into my turf, making a fuss in my town. Falls on me to keep things nice and orderly.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ephyra is not your town. Unless that was your arrangement with the Titan, though the city wasn't his to give away. In any case, ready to earn your nightly keep?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh you don't know the next thing about my arrangement. For one, I always take my pay up front. But with you here... I get a little bonus.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's become of the other Cyclopes? Your kind once aided the gods against the Titans, crafting weapons of war and such. Yet you turn your back...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you think we all keep in touch just 'cause we got one eye at most? What they did and what happened to 'em don't matter to me. 'Specially not when I'm hungry...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I try to watch my diet. Three square meals, that's it. No snacks. You're making it real difficult for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Where I am from, we fast under the waxing moon, and grow nutritious plants and funguses. I could bring you some next time if you would like?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I can't just save you for later. You'd try to run. They always do. Oh, I know! I'll just start with the legs!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I made those docks back there with my bare hands. Know some guys, they tally every skiff and trireme in the drink. We know where you're going.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So what if you do? You're just another servant of Chronos. The gods shall punish you when all this is over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ehh, what do you know about it? They only care about their own! I'm just a joke to them. Am I a joke... to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Finally! Been so long since last I tried to eat ya that the sheep were starting to think you left us high and dry out here. Where'd you go anyways?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you believe it if I told you I've been delving deep into my dreams? You end up fast asleep a lot, so you must know a thing or two of dreaming at this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yeah. Although my dreams are pretty simple nowadays. Eating a good meal, taking a good nap, stuff like that. Come to think, I'm living the dream right now, aren't I?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Are you the one responsible for that spectral barrier at the city gates? It has the markings of Chronos all over it, and you seem to be the extra backup in case it fails.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh that thing, yeah. I knew it'd never hold. But they keep fixing it anyways! Almost like somebody out there don't want you here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see I'm busy over here? Personally, I can't see a thing. Do I complain? No. I just tend my flock.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your blasted sheep are some of the most high-strung animals I have ever encountered.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Sheep are fast. It's not an easy job. And it's even harder when somebody makes a ruckus in my meadow. So what choice do I got? Besides eatin' you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You coulda had my friendship. A couple of my sheep. But instead? You spit in my eye.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have done no such thing. I merely need access to the docks. Yet you insist on getting in my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Put yourself in my sandals. What would you do? You'd say: I don't like the way you're treating me. I am gonna eat you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How is it you detain me every time, yet the mighty Heracles can rampage through the city all he likes and never has to deal with you? Or so he says.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: It's called a double standard, little sheep. Don't need my eye to know he's way bigger than you. You can knock me out for a nice short nap; he can knock me into next week. No thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Funny. Running into you again and again like this? It's made for some of the best sleep I got in years.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is that so? Well it would be even easier for me to knock you out cold if you didn't fight back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, the fight's what does the trick. I don't give it my all, I don't get that deep relaxing slumber afterwards. Or the opportunity to get a hearty meal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know, if you've been having trouble sleeping, there's got to be a better solution than these repeated drubbings from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I tried a lot of stuff. Sleeping on a full stomach, sleeping on an empty stomach. Nothing worked, but this? This works just fine for now...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For all these nights we've fought, you don't even know my name, do you, Cyclops? I'm still nothing but a piece of meat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you said it, not me. Besides, it's not like I'm the one stomping in here, frightening my sheep... well no I guess I do some of the time, but still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Somethin' nasty in the air tonight. And it's not just you or the stink from town. Must be that witch up to no good. You palling up with her?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you know of Lady Medea then you must know she's not somebody to be crossed. She would not hesitate to curse my foes; including you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Was that a threat? I think it was. You don't go saying stuff like that, not in this town. Your pal knows to stay where I can't smell her, unlike you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I know your scent but don't got any real clue what you look like. Probably you're kind of like this shepherd's crook I got between my teeth. No meat on you at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps if you knew my appearance you'd be less inclined to treat me as livestock. My hair is pale as wheat; I'm clad in saffron and silver; and I wield the Nocturnal Arms, forged in the depths of Chaos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmm, wheat and saffron! Throw in some sheep's milk, pinch of sea-salt, little of this, little of that? Then throw it in the fire for a bit... and you'll have bread to die for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Living here, it has its ups and downs. Lately for instance I don't get a lot of peace, though I sleep like a rock. I don't know, maybe I ought to move...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wholeheartedly support that choice. Aren't you more accustomed to island living anyhow? This city life, even on the outskirts here... the pace is far too quick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you want me to do. Although I miss the island life now that you bring it up. Still got unwanted company sometimes but not so much as now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Who told you you could call me by my name, eh? I never introduced myself to you. What, you gonna start calling me Poly next like I'm your pal?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't imagine you have many pals with how you go on trying to eat anything that moves. Though, we could still be friends! Put all that trying-to-eat-me behind us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah! Got all the pals I need. The sheep back there, dead below the dirt down here. You know what I do need, though? Yeah I think you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You know who's disrespected me like this? Nobody. Nobody took my sheep. Took my eye.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well, sometimes we've no one to blame but ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody's gonna pay for all that someday. But you're gonna pay right now. Heh. Metaphorically, I mean! I'm not gonna take your money. Just gonna eat ya... bones and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said nobody took your eye before, as well as your sheep. How did you lose them, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody played a dirty trick on me, is how. Bunch of sailors thought they could get away with my sheep, so I ate a couple of 'em. The sailors, that is! But Nobody got me when I wasn't looking. Then he got away...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And you've sought vengeance on this Nobody ever since?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, not me personally! Someday, somebody's gonna get Nobody for what he did. Though, I am gonna get you right here and now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: No wonder you and Nobody are pals. He and his rats are the only ones that ever got away from me, other than you. That worthless good-for-nothing put you up to this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His name is Odysseus! He's a great hero and tactician, though you needn't worry about slaying him, for he's long dead already. Not your problem anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't matter if he's dead, I'll eat him anyways. And don't care what he calls himself. He's always gonna be a Nobody to me. Now how 'bout I send him a message?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody's not with you this time, is he? Good at running away, that guy. Knew I'd seen the last of him, found out I smelled the last of him, and maybe now I heard the last of him too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't think Odysseus plans to make a habit of visiting, no. Which means you won't ever get to hear what happened when he first escaped from you. It's quite a tale!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I can live with that. Nobody's a dirty sneak... who's gonna want a story about a guy like him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff, Sniff> I never forget a scent, you know. And you got somethin' that's familiar to me from way back when. I know those Bones. Nobody had 'em before you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You must mean these Knuckle Bones... if they've ever been a source of ill fortune for you before, then perhaps you know what's coming.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody's your pal, is that what you expect me to believe? Then first I'm gonna crush your bones, and then I'll eat 'em. And those Knuckle Bones, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey meat, smells to me like you brought those Knuckle Bones again. Nobody really gave you those, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ask him yourself, Cyclops. Nobody wants to speak with you. Heed now my voice, Odysseus; one whom you sought is here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody... can't smell ya for nothing but that's your voice, all right. Why not come a little closer so I can hear ya better?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm afraid you won't be able to consume me in my current state, Cyclops, because I'm long since dead. A mere projection of a Shade, but I did wish to have a word!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, ya really are already dead. Always thought I'd kill ya myself. But ya got the last laugh again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not here to laugh, but to ask you a question. Back when you imprisoned me and my men, killing several of us, you ended up losing your one eye whilst we escaped... was it worth it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I don't remember most meals. Think that's pretty normal. But I remember your crew... tough meat, aged under the sun and all. But worth my eye? Nah, wasn't the best trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor was it good for me, or them. Well anyhow! It seems we each gave one another lasting, bitter scars. Though mine is merely psychological. I'll leave you to the Princess now, you murderous bastard.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Shove off, pal. I didn't ever want to see you again anyways.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: So how is it over there, across the drink? Heard it's been snowing. Sounds like a real rotten place to live.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean Mount Olympus? The gods themselves quite like it, enough to want to fiercely defend it from the Titan you decided to support. But, yes... it's rather cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I could never settle in a place like that. I like the temperature same way I like my meat, nice and hot. 'Course I can also eat it raw. Can't be too picky, and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I know when somethin's off, and somethin's off. What are you up to, meat? Other than getting caught by me like every other time you tried to sneak through here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm not sneaking; in fact I'm here for you this time, Cyclops. The Origin of All Existence is interested to see if I have what it takes to lay you to rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Origin of All Existence...? You took a job to take me out, that's what I'm hearing from you? Well, you wouldn't be the first to try it. Now let me demonstrate what happened to the rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey! The Origin of All Existence wanted me to let you know, you're just not cuttin' it. And the whole deal is off. I also get to eat you now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I find it exceedingly difficult to believe that Primordial Chaos would have been in contact with you about such matters. Easier to believe this is all part of the current Trial.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: If this is all part of some Trial, that makes me the judge. And I hereby declare you're dinner. That is unless you or your pal Primordial Chaos plan to do somethin' about it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Just wanted to be left alone. Move to the countryside, quiet life of the shepherd and all. Only for you to drag me out of retirement.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've done no such thing. I'm going to the docks. You're choosing to try and stop me, when you could be tending to your sheep instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'll tend to them all right. But in order to do that, I got to tend to the likes of you first. Just like I used to in the good old days.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very obstinate for a peaceful shepherd. Did Poseidon do something to offend you at some point? Is that what this is about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't get offended... I get even. And you don't get to ask questions no more... you get eaten.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Last I'll say about Poseidon, I swear. He's my uncle, you know. For what it's worth... I'm sorry if he truly led you to believe you were his son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well, I like to play up the family resemblance just in case. Though I'm glad he's not my pop neither. Otherwise I'd be about to eat my own cousin, and that'd be real weird.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: The dead... they talk sometimes, or try. Nothin' better to do around here. Know what they're going on about? I couldn't make it out at first, but sounds an awful lot like... Prometheus.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Titan of Foresight, and one of your allies, in case you've not met; for apparently you serve the same master. What have the wretched dead to say about the likes of him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Mostly just moans and groans. Might be they're hungry like me! 'Cause if Prometheus really made off with fire from the gods like I'm hearin'... he probably knows how to cook.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Been finding if I talk too much, it messes with my sleep. Maybe we ought to cut with the chat and go straight to it. But I don't mean to be rude, so you... got any last words, meat?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Certainly, my last words are: You're never going to eat me, and you're never going to stop me, but you're welcome to keep trying, and cheers for all the Wool!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, hold on... so you're the one who's been stealing all my Wool this whole time? Aw, now that does it...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you care so much about your sheep, why keep putting them in harm's way? Not to mention eating them. Let them leave here, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Scuse me? I didn't ask you for your shepherding advice, and besides... these sheep got nowhere else to be. And we're the dinner entertainment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hairs on the back of my neck all standin' up, and not 'cause of you. Or maybe it is, but... it's like I got a mark on me. Not had this feeling in a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nothing personal, Cyclops. But the Testament of Night requires your defeat. You needn't concern yourself with the inner workings of it, so how about we just fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Testament of what? I didn't get a damn word you said at the beginnin' there, but you got me on board at just fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: So you can mess with how things work at night? Between the feeling in my gut and the nonsense you've been talkin', I can tell you've been up to no good. You some kind of witch?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea isn't the only one to practice around here. Since you're finally wising up to our true strength, know that you violate the will of Night herself by standing in my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: See, there you go again with all the fancy talk. But hey, I could go for a fancy meal just as soon as a tough piece of meat! Maybe you're both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: So you can mess with how things work at night? Between the feeling in my gut and the nonsense you've been talkin', I can tell you've been up to no good. You some kind of witch?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Since you're finally wising up to our true strength, know that you violate the will of Night herself by standing in my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: See, there you go again with all the fancy talk. But hey, I could go for a fancy meal just as soon as a tough piece of meat! Maybe you're both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Not heard you show up in a while! Glad you're back. Been gone so long it's really been eatin' into my sleep schedule.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For a moment there, I thought you were genuinely pleased at my return. But you just want me to render you unconscious.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. That or stand still long enough for me to eat you up. But you don't seem interested in that, so I'll just go for the unconscious thing. I'm easy to please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Where do you think you're goin', anyways? No ship that sails from the docks back there is gonna take the likes of you. Come to think of it... lately, we've had no ships at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The deathless fleet of Chronos is still out there in the waters of the Rift. Apparently when one ship sinks, another rises to replace it from the depths, drowned sailors for its crew...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You're pulling my leg. The dead can sail nowadays? I thought they were no good for anything but food. Might have to teach 'em to take on some of my household chores...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, where you been? My sheep started getting all restless here these past couple of nights. Guess they've gotten used to having you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's very considerate of them, though I had other business to take care of, else I would have paid them a visit. Could you arrange for me to meet them but not you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Nah. My sheep and I are what you call a package deal. The kind of deal I figured you wouldn't pass up for too long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I remember back when first we met, you seemed enormous... much bigger than most any other foe I'd ever faced. But now, you're hardly any match for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, thanks. And I remember back when first we met, my sleeping schedule was completely out of whack! But it's all better now. And I've still got my figure! Enormous, was it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's all a matter of perspective, as I've learned. For instance, you're quite minuscule in comparison to the Father of All Monsters, Typhon. He's properly enormous.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll have to take your word for it. Anyways just how big can he be? No more than three square meals' worth if I had to guess. Been too long since I had some monster meat...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You some kind of deathless goddess of the Underworld, you said? Seems you'd be better off staying on your side of the fence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm here to pay my relatives a visit. You just keep getting in the way. Almost as though violence better suits you than the shepherd's life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you're a real thinker for a slice of meat! Just 'cause I'm retired don't mean I don't want to work up an appetite no more, does it now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff> You stink like the dead. But you're not from the town. You're from further below. You'd be better off staying on your side of the fence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm here to pay my relatives a visit. You just keep getting in the way. Almost as though violence better suits you than the shepherd's life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you're a real thinker for a slice of meat! Just 'cause I'm retired don't mean I don't want to work up an appetite no more, does it now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> Look, I'm just a humble shepherd trying to live in peace. But I gotta protect my own, you understand. Plus, I gotta eat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You call this trying to live in peace?! Perhaps you shouldn't be interfering with my efforts to put an end to this war raging all around us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: The dead don't bother me. It's the living always been a pain in my behind. Good thing the living taste better than the dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You know it's nice here with this witch around. I'm normally not big on talk, more about the olfactory, and the auditory, and all that. So I might let her handle the conversatin' for a bit...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Well here we are already, sorceress! Mere moments prior we were speaking amicably by my cauldron, and yet now, it's come to this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you're to confront me here because of the Oath we took, Lady Medea, but the Cyclops hasn't had as good of an excuse...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Medea_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, you've trespassed on his property, for one, and he is famished, for another! Look at him, he's at a loss for words. Lord Polyphemus, you must say something!

endLines.speaker: Polyphemus
endLines.text: How 'bout I let my feet do the talkin' this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Almost got me last time, but not quite. Come to finish the job? You wouldn't be the first to try. But I'm still here, and you don't see anybody else around, do ya?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Finish what job? You're not my mark. I'd just as soon slip past you and be on my way, except your blasted sense of smell and hearing are too good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, thanks. And you know how I keep 'em top-notch like this, don't ya? When you got gifts like these, you gotta put 'em to good use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: You frightened my sheep. That's a real no-no in this town.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I apologize for my transgression, Cyclops. What do I owe you for the inconvenience? I can pay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you'll pay. The punishment's gettin' eaten alive. By me. Sorry, I don't make the rules.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That seems unusually strict. There's no use following a senseless code of law.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah, only kiddin'! I do make the rules. And I get real tired only eatin' sheep.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow! Just when I thought I had you last time, you gave me the slip. Didn't think I'd get another shot.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You'll soon have many more such shots. But you're going to fail every time, I promise you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: How 'bout you promise me the meat on your bones. Though I get the feeling you don't got much to offer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't like playing with my food. Like it even less when my food plays with me. I'm on to that disappearing act of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am a deathless goddess of the Underworld; the shadows will always take me before you will. Yet you would fight me endlessly anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh yeah! Everybody slips up once in a while. Even you. And when you do... we'll find out just how deathless you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff> I smell blood. I was just getting ready to prepare my next meal... but the town welcoming committee must have prepped you already.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The welcoming committee, as you call it, are servants of Chronos... and shall suffer for their arrogance. You would cast your lot with them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I used to have a little trouble eating food that looked back at me, or talked back at me. But I don't got that problem no more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, I can tell the town welcoming committee must have done a number on you already. Softened you up good. All the better for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The city's filled with the unburied dead. I can slay them but they merely rise again; they're very troublesome. The same as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't you get testy. This is my pasture not yours. And every little sheep around here answers to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Stepped out of line again, little sheep. You know what happens next.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sniff> Somethin' stinks... oh I know what it is. It's you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: No sneaking past my sheep. No sneaking past me, neither.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: One of these nights... I'm gonna catch you. And I'm gonna eat you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: It's a little early for my dinnertime, but hey! I can be flexible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Phew! Know I could smell you from a league away?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You just don't know what's good for you, do you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You want some more of this, that's fine by me. Oh, I got plenty.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't normally like eating my food raw. But then, I like to try new stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Shh! Think I heard somethin'. You hear it, right? The dinner bell...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, what do you know? It's dinnertime already...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: All this shepherding all day works up a real appetite...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome back to the flock, little sheep. Now, come 'ere...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Got myself a good night's rest, but not enough to eat...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to get to those docks, I want to eat you. Just the way it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Do remember to watch for falling rocks in this town.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Here comes some dead meat, fresh from out of town...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I really got to do somethin' about that wall you keep on getting through...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You're back for some more shepherding, huh, little sheep?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: No one gets in this town, no one gets out; those are the rules.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to hand it to ya. I've been sleeping like a baby lately over here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: After a hard day's shepherding, I could go for a good knock-out about now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if I can't eat you, least I'll get some decent sleep here in a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess it's almost my bedtime. But first, a little bit of exercise...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Kick you right into the Moon right now if I could see the thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Really want to get stomped on some more, I'm good for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to end up like my sheep, you keep this up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, you just wait'll I get these hands on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Little early for my dinnertime, but then, I shouldn't eat so late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: You step onto my turf, you pay the price, that's just the rules.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Last time you knocked me out so fast, got the best sleep I had in a while!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't even get a piece of you last time, but you can't get away forever...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This isn't going to last, you know, Cyclops. At some point, the City of Ephyra shall be rebuilt. Its streets will be teeming with mortals, the docks bustling with seafarers... rather than this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you, an oracle or somethin'? If they try and put the town back together, great. More meat for me whenever I want! You'll have to do for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your service to the Titan Chronos is complete, Cyclops! His war against Olympus is ended if you've not already heard. I hold no enmity toward you. Be on your way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I was just wondering how come I haven't gotten paid. Thought maybe my shepherding hasn't been up to snuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos was compensating you for tending sheep? I thought you were supposed to be guarding the city gates.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you even know about it. You really think I'm gonna leave my flock now, after all we've been through? And I can always earn my meal ticket some other way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see you decided not to act on my prior recommendation, for the good of your flock, whose sheep you often eat. For how much longer do you plan to bar me from the Rift?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Thinking it might be a while. Got my flock, got my barn, been sleeping better lately... and having to uproot? Settle someplace else? Packing, and unpacking, I don't want any of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How fortunate you've made yourself such a comfortable arrangement that happens to be right in my blasted way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, that's just another perk: I even get my nightly exercise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: How come you said whatever war is over, huh? The town's about the same. Last I checked the dead are meant to be underground, not walking through the streets.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The war with Chronos is over, it's just... there are a few remaining details being sorted out, and the Underworld is in enough of a state of disrepair that it isn't back in order yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, that's too bad. I'd tell you to go down there and help 'em out, but that might cut into my sleep routine, which has been pretty good lately. Now come on, it's almost bedtime here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hey! I wanted to say thanks. Gave me a real zinger there last time, you know that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're back. And you're grateful that I vanquished you earlier? You seem more the sort to hold a grudge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: I am, I am, it's just... I hadn't had a good night's sleep in I-don't-know-how-long. After you knocked me out cold, I woke up feeling more refreshed than ever! And real hungry, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch! Don't even try and sneak by. What, you think you were gonna catch me napping here? Not gonna happen.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It might. If you keep blocking my path, you'll get another thrashing, and you'll be sound asleep again in no time. That what you want?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cyclops_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Got a dilemma on my hands! I'm always sleepy, yeah. But I'm always hungry, too. Guess that makes this a can't-lose proposition.
